# UC-22.11.51 · Inactive Human Accounts Still Entitled to CDE Groups

## Description

Joins last successful logon timestamps to current CDE group membership so dormant identities are removed before they become an easy target for misuse of credentials.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes windows and sourcetypes ad:user_snapshot. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Build LDAP snapshot forwarder with `lastLogonTimestamp`. (2) Align 90-day threshold to corporate policy. (3) Auto-disable workflow in IAM integration. (4) Monthly PCI access review attachment. Indexes required: `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ad:user_snapshot`; Lookups: `pci_cde_ad_groups`, `pci_cde_ad_groups.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Daily `sourcetype="ad:user_snapshot"` HEC; `lookup pci_cde_ad_groups.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Build LDAP snapshot forwarder with `lastLogonTimestamp`. (2) Align 90-day threshold to corporate policy. (3) Auto-disable workflow in IAM integration. (4) Monthly PCI access review attachment.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="ad:user_snapshot" earliest=-2d
| lookup pci_cde_ad_groups.csv memberOf OUTPUT in_cde_group
| where in_cde_group="true"
| eval idle_days=round((now()-lastLogonTimestamp)/86400,1)
| where idle_days>90 AND NOT match(samAccountName,"(?i)^svc-")
| table samAccountName, idle_days, lastLogonTimestamp, department, manager
| sort - idle_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Inactive Human Accounts Still Entitled to CDE Groups (PCI DSS Req 8.2.5, 8.2.6)** — Joins last successful logon timestamps to current CDE group membership so dormant identities are removed before they become an easy target for misuse of credentials.

Documented **Data sources**: Daily `sourcetype="ad:user_snapshot"` HEC; `lookup pci_cde_ad_groups.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows; **sourcetype**: ad:user_snapshot. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=windows, sourcetype="ad:user_snapshot", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where in_cde_group="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **idle_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where idle_days>90 AND NOT match(samAccountName,"(?i)^svc-")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: department, idle_days, lastLogonTimestamp, manager, samAccountName.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (idle users), bar (idle_days), single value (count stale CDE users).

## SPL

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="ad:user_snapshot" earliest=-2d
| lookup pci_cde_ad_groups.csv memberOf OUTPUT in_cde_group
| where in_cde_group="true"
| eval idle_days=round((now()-lastLogonTimestamp)/86400,1)
| where idle_days>90 AND NOT match(samAccountName,"(?i)^svc-")
| table samAccountName, idle_days, lastLogonTimestamp, department, manager
| sort - idle_days
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (idle users), bar (idle_days), single value (count stale CDE users).
